skill.name: skills-creator
skill.description: Creates and maintains Agent Skills with concise SKILL.md files, trigger descriptions, templates, and supporting references.
skill.summary: Guides creation and maintenance of Agent Skills using templates, references, and progressive disclosure.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk hits, but review found they are mostly Markdown examples, file names, documentation links, and local skill-management commands. The real risk is moderate because the skill instructs an agent to create and edit Skill files under .claude/skills, and its references include shell command examples that users may copy. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, hidden network behavior, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Agent Skill File Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to create supporting resources and save SKILL.md files under .claude/skills. This is expected behavior for a skill authoring tool, but it can modify agent configuration files and should be used only in trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing instruction is explicit and central to the skill. The risk is operational rather than malicious because it is scoped to creating Skill files.

medium_findings.title: Reference Guidance Describes Executable Skill Resources
medium_findings.description: The reference material explains that Skills can include scripts and that agents may execute scripts through bash. This is legitimate documentation, but generated skills should be reviewed before use because future supporting files may run commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly discusses executable resources and bash execution. No bundled executable script exists in this package, so the concern depends on skills created later.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The many external command detections are Markdown examples for creating, listing, testing, committing, and removing skills. They are not automatically executed by this skill, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside fenced Markdown documentation. Review confirmed the destructive example is scoped to a sample skill directory, not root or the full home directory.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Skill Paths Are Documented
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged ~/.claude paths as hidden home-directory access. These paths are the documented personal Skill location and appear as examples, not secret file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are visible documentation for Claude Code skill storage. There is no evidence of credential harvesting or hidden file enumeration.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL detections are links to Claude and Anthropic documentation. They do not send local data or make network requests from code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown link text. No executable fetch, curl, webhook, or data transfer behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and C2 Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography and C2 keyword detections appear to match ordinary Markdown text, file extensions, examples, and documentation terms. No cryptographic implementation, command-and-control endpoint, or malware behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation context at the cited files and no code implementing weak algorithms or C2 behavior. Confidence is high, but not perfect because the static list is very noisy.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scoped Recursive Delete Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes rm -rf examples for deleting sample skill directories. This is not malicious, but recursive deletion commands are inherently dangerous if copied and modified incorrectly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command is present in documentation and is scoped to ~/.claude/skills/my-skill and .claude/skills/my-skill. It is dangerous only if misused outside that scope.

content.user_title: Create Agent Skills for Claude and Codex
content.value_statement: Building reliable agent skills is hard when triggers, structure, and supporting files are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through concise skill authoring with templates and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Creates SKILL.md files with valid YAML frontmatter and concise instructions.
Uses a bundled template to structure workflows, rules, and acceptance criteria.
Applies Agent Skill best practices for descriptions, triggers, and progressive disclosure.
Guides placement of personal or project skills under .claude/skills paths.
Helps add reference files, scripts, or templates only when they are necessary.
Checks naming, description length, terminology, and basic skill acceptance criteria.
limitations: Does not validate generated skills with an external marketplace service.
Does not guarantee that generated scripts or resources are secure.
Requires the user or agent to review filesystem changes before use.
Focuses on Claude-style Agent Skills, not arbitrary plugin frameworks.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated personal workflow into a reusable skill with clear triggers and a concise SKILL.md file.

use_cases.title: Document Team Project Conventions
use_cases.description: Package project-specific review, testing, or release guidance as a shared skill stored in the project repository.

use_cases.title: Refine Existing Skill Instructions
use_cases.description: Improve an existing skill by tightening the description, reducing context load, and adding focused reference files.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill from a simple workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Agent Skill for [workflow]. It should activate when users ask for [trigger]. Keep the SKILL.md concise.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes Into a Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using existing process notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these process notes into an Agent Skill. Create a strong description, workflow, rules, and acceptance criteria: [notes].

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Draft Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Review and revise an existing skill draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md for trigger quality, progressive disclosure, length, and conflicting instructions. Rewrite it with focused improvements: [draft].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Skill Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a skill with supporting resources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete skill package for [domain]. Decide which references, templates, or scripts are necessary, and explain the final file structure.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for generating release notes from merged pull requests.
output_examples.output: A concise release-notes skill with a valid name, activation description, workflow steps, rules for source review, and acceptance checks.

output_examples.input: Improve this long SKILL.md so it loads less context.
output_examples.output: A shorter skill body, a clearer trigger description, and recommendations for moving detailed examples into reference files.

output_examples.input: Make a project skill for code review conventions.
output_examples.output: A project-ready skill plan with a SKILL.md outline, focused review criteria, and guidance for storing it under .claude/skills.

best_practices: Keep the description specific, short, and focused on when the skill should activate.
Move long examples or domain references into separate files that are read only when needed.
Review generated filesystem changes before enabling or sharing a new skill.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that overlap with many unrelated workflows.
Do not add scripts or dependencies unless deterministic execution is necessary.
Do not include time-sensitive facts that will become stale inside the skill.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or improves Agent Skill packages, especially SKILL.md files with clear triggers, workflows, and acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The included references describe Claude Code personal and project Skill locations.

faq.question: Can it create supporting files?
faq.answer: Yes. It may suggest references, scripts, or templates when they are useful for progressive disclosure.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium risk warning because it guides filesystem changes and includes shell command examples.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No bundled executable code was found. The references contain command examples that users or agents may choose to run.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Use it if you create, maintain, or review Agent Skills for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code workflows.
